---
title: 'Expendable'
date: '2014-12-31T14:19:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '4805843567108927085'
blogger_filename: '/2014/12/expendable_31.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'White Noise'
---

<br /><br /><br /><br /><h3 style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><b>Black Suit,</b> real<br />name unknown</h3><br /><ul style="direction: ltr; margin-bottom: 0in; margin-left: .375in; margin-top: 0in; unicode-bidi: embed;" type="disc"><br /><li style="margin-bottom: 0; margin-top: 0; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt;">Alias: Thaddeus J. B.<br />     Rotcudnoc</span></li><br /><li style="margin-bottom: 0; margin-top: 0; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt;">Alias:</span></li><br /><li style="margin-bottom: 0; margin-top: 0; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt;">Alias:</span></li><br /><li style="margin-bottom: 0; margin-top: 0; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt;">Alias:</span></li><br /></ul><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><h4 style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-weight: bold;">Motivation: Power/Control, to be the apex of every<br />triangle, and the conductor of every interaction. </span></h4><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Thaddeus abhors<br />social inventions. He is a trillion-aire+++. His net personal (non-corporate) net<br />worth is equal, at least, to the annual GDP of North America combined. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />He needs to be in<br />control of all things at all times. Money is not a motivation, it is a tool<br />used to achieve that control. He owns half the US Senators, and blackmails<br />another 1/4 of them. He considers Warren Buffet a small time nuisance, and<br />sometimes an unwitting participant in his schemes. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Real power is<br />wielded secretly, quietly, and firmly. He never uses his real name, he's not<br />even sure if he can remember his real name. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Today he's decided<br />to be known as Thaddeus.</div><br /><br /><br />&nbsp;-----------------------------------<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><div style="border-width: 100%; direction: ltr;"><br /><br /><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 4.2597in;"><br /><br /><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 4.2597in;"><br /><br /><h4 style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-weight: bold;">Thaddeus Point Of View</span></h4><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-style: italic;">Thaddeus, we cannot push him any further, he's<br />already been in the room longer than most others, and his vitals are<br />dangerously out of control. We must put an end to this test… or give him a rest<br />and start again. </span></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-style: italic;">You Dr. Briar, like these experiments, are temporary<br />and expendable…<span style="mso-spacerun: yes;">&nbsp; </span>you would do well to<br />remember that. </span></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />He enjoyed seeing<br />that look on people's face, when they first realize that they were never the<br />one in charge or in control. When they realize that the person they thought<br />they brought on to assist, was actually the one calling the shots. What they<br />seldom know, and could not understand, is that they were never calling the<br />shots. They were all part of his orchestrated plan. They were but a violin in<br />his symphony, and easily replaceable. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Thaddeus broke his<br />stare when his point had been thoroughly impressed on his compatriots face… <span style="font-style: italic;">I'll be in the observation lounge that I paid so much<br />money to build. If we lose him, I will handle it. Proceed.</span> </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />He stepped out of<br />the room, and walked down the hall to the observation room. He wanted to see<br />this for himself.<span style="mso-spacerun: yes;">&nbsp; </span>This one showed so<br />much more promise than the others. The new candidate criteria were working out<br />quite nicely. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />************</div><br /></div><br /></div><br /></div><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><div style="border-width: 100%; direction: ltr;"><br /><br /><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 4.2597in;"><br /><br /><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 4.2597in;"><br /><br /><h4 style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-weight: bold;">Dr. Briar Point Of View</span></h4><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-style: italic;">Thaddeus, we cannot push him any further, he's<br />already been in the room longer than most others, and his vitals are<br />dangerously out of control. We must put an end to this test… or give him a rest<br />and start again. </span></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><span style="font-style: italic;">You Dr. Briar, like these experiments, are temporary<br />and expendable…<span style="mso-spacerun: yes;">&nbsp; </span>you would do well to<br />remember that. </span></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />A chill crept over<br />his spine, from his tail bone, up his neck, and into his head.. His skin became<br />cold as the realization dawned on him… He was never the one in control. When<br />he'd brought Thaddeus on, he'd been so hungry for the financial backing and the<br />seeming eagerness that he showed in the project, he'd never stopped to consider<br />that Thaddeus would take over. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />…<span style="font-style: italic;">I'll be in the observation lounge that I paid so much<br />money to build. If we lose him, I will handle it. Proceed.</span> Thaddeus<br />stepped out of the room, and walked down the hall.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Dr. Briar slumped<br />into his chair with the realization… he'd sold his science, his soul, to this<br />partnership. Without this financial backing, he'd have to pull out and stop<br />everything. Shameful though it may be, the scientist in him was curious to see<br />what would happen if he pushed the patient over the age. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />So he turned back to<br />the monitor, and kept one on the vitals, then turned pushed the slider one<br />notch higher. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />-------------------------------------------------------------------------</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><h4 style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><b>Scene</b>: </h4><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Dr. Briar is in the<br />control room, watching the night vision monitor and vitals. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Thaddeus is in the<br />observation room, watching the same monitors and vitals on a large flat screen.<br />He's sitting in very expensive leather chairs with cup holders. The room is<br />equipped like a studio apartment, kitchen, living room, on suite. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />Ash is sitting in a<br />dentist chair in the middle of a pitch black room, observable through night<br />vision cameras. The room is outlined in chicken wire, and sound absorbing<br />material lines all sides, above, and beneath. The walls, ceiling, and floor are<br />lined with this material for a depth of 15 feet each, and backed by other<br />layers of non-reflective material, before being enclosed in 100's of feet of<br />concrete and plastic. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />A sound box is<br />playing inside the room; however, it is putting out frequencies above and below<br />the human audible spectrum. Ash is unaware that any "sound" is being<br />produced. These frequencies are known to have an effect on the body and mind,<br />but are not detectable to the human ear, and are therefore audibly invisible.<br />These frequencies are known to be particularly interesting for their occurrence<br />and commonality in observable nature, and their effect on the their<br />surroundings. Today, these are being played at various decibel levels and the<br />frequencies themselves are being altered and fine-tuned, constantly compared to<br />the vitals on the screens, in order to see what affects these tunings cause. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />When a frequency is<br />played through a speaker, and there is reverberation in the room (sound<br />bouncing off of the walls) the harmonies of that frequency will also be picked<br />up in a recording of that room. Without the reverberation effect, this room can<br />test the single frequency and/or any variation of the harmony frequencies they<br />wish to test. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />They have discovered<br />that the frequency ranges of 3Hz, 7 Hz, and 12Hz have produced intriguing<br />results… others have also produces interesting results, but not always<br />positive, usable, results. </div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />------------------------------------------------------------------------</div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11.0pt; margin: 0in;"><br />*******</div><br /></div><br /></div><br /></div><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />
